_meta.note: This file is a *seed*. The GitHub Action at .github/workflows/refresh-docs.yml regenerates it from a real PSRCHIVE install by scraping `<command> --help` output (see tools/scrape_help.py). Anything authored by hand below the per-command 'desc' / 'long' fields should survive regeneration — the parser only overwrites 'flags', 'usage', and 'help_raw'.

template.label: Templates
template.blurb: Build the standard profile for timing.

timing.label: Timing
timing.blurb: Generate TOAs and feed timing tools.

commands.long: Project the 4-D archive onto any pair of axes — phase vs frequency, phase vs time, the Stokes parameters, polarisation angle, the integrated profile. Combine with -j operations to scrunch and dedisperse on the fly.
commands.name: psrplot

flags.v: metafile contains list of archive filenames
plot dimensions in pixels, width times height
loop over the range of the named parameter
set the colour indeces to loop over

commands.help_raw:
psrplot - pulsar plotting program

usage: psrplot [options] filename[s] 

where options are:

  -h,-help          print this help page
  -i,-version       version information
  -q                quiet mode
  -v                verbose mode
  -V                very verbose mode
  -M metafile       metafile contains list of archive filenames
  -config file      configuration file

Plot device options:
  -D device         plot to the specified device
  -g WxH            plot dimensions in pixels, width times height
  -N XxY            plot panels, width by height
  -r ratio          aspect ratio (height/width)
  -w width          plot surface width (in centimetres)

Preprocessing options:
  -j commands       execute pulsar shell preprocessing commands
  -J script         execute pulsar shell preprocessing script

  -P                Help: list available plot types
  -C plot           Help: list options specific to 'plot'
  -A plot           Help: list common options for 'plot'

  -p plot           add plot type
  -c cfg[s]         add plot options
  -s style          add plot options in 'style' file

  -x                disable default preprocessing
  -S                stack plots
  -O                overlay plots
  -F                overlay plots from multiple files

  -l name=<range>   loop over the range of the named parameter
  -lci <range>      set the colour indeces to loop over

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/psrplot for more details

commands.long: The original PGPLOT-based plotter. Largely superseded by psrplot but still common in pipelines because its flags are short.
commands.name: pav

flags.v: Centre the pulse peak at phase 0.5
Rotate all profiles by phase (in turns)
Zoom into the data between 0 and max (normalized coords)
Read a meta-file containing the files to use
Zoom to this pulse phase range (in turns)
Zoom to this position angle range (Degrees)
Divide the window into x by y panels
Select which frequency channel to display
Plot a single profile (chan 0, poln 0, subint 0 by default)
Plot an image of amplitude against frequency & phase
Display a simple dynamic spectrum image
Plot Poincare vector in spherical coordinates
Plot polarization parameters (I,L,V,PA) with an intermediate cropped flux plot
Plot cal amplitude and phase vs frequency channel
Plot colour map of sub-integrations against pulse phase
Stack labels(name,freq) on the left side of the plot
Publication quality plot (B&W)  L dashed, V dots
Publication quality plot (keep in colour if device supports it)
Select a colour map for PGIMAG style plots
Plot Stokes Q and Stokes U in '-S' option instead of degree of linear
Plot an error box, for -D and -S
Plot position angle plot from -90 deg to 180 deg, instead of -90,90
Plot versus Hour Angle in '-Y' mode

commands.help_raw:
Preprocessing options:
 -b scr    Bscrunch scr phase bins together
 -C        Centre the pulse peak at phase 0.5
 -d        Dedisperse all channels
 -r phase  Rotate all profiles by phase (in turns)
 -f scr    Fscrunch scr frequency channels together
 -F        Fscrunch all frequency channels
 -t src    Tscrunch scr Integrations together
 -T        Tscrunch all Integrations
 -p        Add all polarisations together
 -x  max   Zoom into the data between 0 and max (normalized coords)
 -c        Do not remove baseline

Configuration options:
 -g dev    Manually specify a plot device
 -M meta   Read a meta-file containing the files to use

Selection options:
 -z x1,x2  Zoom to this pulse phase range (in turns)
 -k f1,f2  Zoom to this frequency range
 -y s1,s2  Zoom to this subint range
 -l a1,a2  Zoom to this position angle range (Degrees)
 -N x,y    Divide the window into x by y panels
 -H chan   Select which frequency channel to display
 -P pol    Select which polarization to display
 -I subint Select which sub-integration to display

Plotting options:
 -B        Plot original bandpass
 -J        Plot off-pulse bandpass & channel weights
 -D        Plot a single profile (chan 0, poln 0, subint 0 by default)
 -G        Plot an image of amplitude against frequency & phase
 -K        Plot Digitiser Statistics
 -j        Display a simple dynamic spectrum image
 -m        Plot Poincare vector in spherical coordinates
 -n        Plot S/N against frequency
 -R        Plot stacked sub-integration profiles
 -S        Plot polarization parameters (I,L,V,PA)
 -E        Plot polarization parameters (I,L,V,PA) with an intermediate cropped flux plot
 -A        Plot Digitiser Counts histogram
 -X        Plot cal amplitude and phase vs frequency channel
 -Y        Plot colour map of sub-integrations against pulse phase

Other plotting options:
 --sl           Stack labels(name,freq) on the left side of the plot
 --publn        Publication quality plot (B&W)  L dashed, V dots
 --publnc       Publication quality plot (keep in colour if device supports it)
 --ch           Set the character height
 --cmap index   Select a colour map for PGIMAG style plots
                The available indices are: (maybe 4-6 not needed)
                  0 -> Greyscale
                  1 -> Inverse Greyscale
                  2 -> Heat
                  3 -> Cold
                  4 -> Plasma
                  5 -> Forest
                  6 -> Alien Glow
                  7 -> CubeHelix
                  8 -> Test

 --plot_qu      Plot Stokes Q and Stokes U in '-S' option instead of degree of linear
 --ld           Label phase axes in degrees
 --ebox         Plot an error box, for -D and -S 
 --pa_ext       Plot position angle plot from -90 deg to 180 deg, instead of -90,90
 --ha           Plot versus Hour Angle in '-Y' mode

Integration re-ordering (nsub = final # of subints) (used with pav Y for binary pulsars):
 --convert_binphsperi   nsub
 --convert_binphsasc    nsub
 --convert_binlngperi   nsub
 --convert_binlngasc    nsub

Utility options:
 -a              Print available plugin information
 -h              Display this useful help page
 -i              Show revision information
 -v              Verbose output
 -V              Very verbose output

commands.long: Modify an archive in place (or with a new extension). The workhorse for time/frequency/phase scrunching, dedispersion, weight adjustment, and metadata fixes.
commands.name: pam

flags.v: Modify the original files on disk
List of archive filenames in metafile
The archive class to create valid types are
Write new files with this extension
Transform to coherence parameters (i.e. undo -S option)
Time scrunch to this many subints
Time scrunch to this subint length
Frequency scrunch to this many channels
Bin scrunch to this many bins
Convert to binary phase periastron order
Convert to binary phase asc node order
Convert to binary longitude periastron order
Convert to binary longitude asc node order
Correct ISM Faraday rotation (wrt centre freq)
Install a new RM (but don't correct)
Rotate profiles by this many turns
Reset profile weights to this value
Install the Receiver described in the text file
Install a new ephemeris and update model
Ephem type for -E, 'ver' should be 'tempo' or 'tempo2'
Flip the band about the given frequency
Reverse the ordering of the frequency channels and
Change the 'type' parameter where 'type' is one of:
Correct 'site' of telescope (One letter tempo code- GBT=1, PKS=7 etc)
Replace header DM with current value from ephemeris

commands.help_raw:
A program for manipulating Pulsar::Archives 
Usage: pam [options] filenames 
  -q               Quiet mode 
  -v               Verbose mode 
  -V               Very verbose mode 
  -i               Show revision information 
  -m               Modify the original files on disk 
  -M metafile      List of archive filenames in metafile 
  -a archive       The archive class to create valid types are 
                   (Timer,Baseband,EPN,ASP,PSRFITS,WAPP,PRESTO,BPP,UVM,ASCII,EPOS,pdv_KtZ) 
  -e extension     Write new files with this extension 
  -u path          Write files to this location 
  -T               Time scrunch to one subint 
  -F               Frequency scrunch to one channel 
  -p               Polarisation scrunch to total intensity 
  -D               Dedisperse (but not fscrunch) 
  --DD             Dededisperse (i.e. undo -D option) 
  -I               Transform to Invariant Interval 
  -S               Transform to Stokes parameters 
  --SS             Transform to coherence parameters (i.e. undo -S option)
  -x "start end"   Extract subints in this inclusive range 

The following options take integer arguments 
  -t               Time scrunch by this factor 
  -f               Frequency scrunch by this factor 
  -b               Bin scrunch by this factor 
  --setnsub        Time scrunch to this many subints 
  --settsub        Time scrunch to this subint length 
  --setnchn        Frequency scrunch to this many channels 
  --setnbin        Bin scrunch to this many bins 
  --binphsperi     Convert to binary phase periastron order 
  --binphsasc      Convert to binary phase asc node order 
  --binlngperi     Convert to binary longitude periastron order 
  --binlngasc      Convert to binary longitude asc node order 

The following options take floating point arguments 
  -d DM            Alter the header dispersion measure 
  -R RM            Correct ISM Faraday rotation (wrt centre freq) 
  --RM RM          Install a new RM (but don't correct) 
  --aux_rm RMi     Correct absolute auxiliary Faraday rotation 
  -s duty          Smear with this duty cycle 
  -r turns         Rotate profiles by this many turns 
  -w weight        Reset profile weights to this value 
  --mult fac       Multiply amplitudes by 'fac' 
  --period period  Install a new folding period 

The following options override archive parameters 
  --receiver file  Install the Receiver described in the text file 
  -L               Set feed basis to Linear 
  -C               Set feed basis to Circular 
  -E ephfile       Install a new ephemeris and update model 
  --ephver ver     Ephem type for -E, 'ver' should be 'tempo' or 'tempo2'
  -B               Flip the sideband sense 
  --flip freq      Flip the band about the given frequency 
  --reverse_freqs  Reverse the ordering of the frequency channels and
                   change the bandwidth flag accordingly
  -o centre_freq   Change the frequency labels 
  --type type      Change the 'type' parameter where 'type' is one of:
                   'Pulsar', 'PolnCal', 'FluxCalOn', 'FluxCalOff', 'Calibrator'
  --inst inst      Change the instrument name 
  --site site      Correct 'site' of telescope (One letter tempo code- GBT=1, PKS=7 etc)
  --name name      Change source name
  --update_dm      Replace header DM with current value from ephemeris

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/pam for more details

commands.long: Mark interference as zero-weight so it's excluded from averages. -r runs the default median filter; manual ranges allow surgical removal.
commands.name: paz

flags.v: metafile contains list of archive filenames
write files with a new extension
write files to a new directory
Zero weight the intersection of -[wW] and -[zZk]
Zero weight chans listed in this kill file
Zero weight this much of each band edge
Zap channels using median smoothed difference
Set the size of the median smoothing window
Mow the lawn of the specified subint
Mow the lawn of all subints
Zero weight chans using mean offset rejection
Zero weight chans based on S/N (std optional)
Zero weight subints with negative dropouts

commands.help_raw:
paz - zaps RFI in archives

usage: paz [options] filename[s] 

where options are:

  -h,-help         print this help page
  -i,-version      version information
  -q               quiet mode
  -v               verbose mode
  -V               very verbose mode
  -M metafile      metafile contains list of archive filenames
  -config file     configuration file

Preprocessing options:
  -j commands      execute pulsar shell preprocessing commands
  -J script        execute pulsar shell preprocessing script

Output options:
  -m               modify (overwrite) the original file
  -e ext           write files with a new extension
  -O path          write files to a new directory

Manual zapping options:
  -I               Zero weight the intersection of -[wW] and -[zZk]
  -k filename      Zero weight chans listed in this kill file
  -B "a b"         Zap pulse phase bins between a and b inclusive
  -z "a b c ..."   Zero weight these particular channels
  -Z "a b"         Zero weight chans between a & b inclusive
  -f "a b c ..."   Zero weight channels with these frequencies (MHz)
  -F "a b"         Zero weight this frequency range, inclusive (MHz)
  -x "a b c ..."   Delete all sub-integrations except these
  -X "a b"         Delete all sub-ints except a to b inclusive
  -E percent       Zero weight this much of each band edge
  -s "a b c ..."   Delete these sub-integrations
  -S "a b"         Delete sub-ints between a & b inclusive
  -w "a b c ..."   Zero weight these sub-integrations
  -W "a b"         Zero weight sub-ints between a & b inclusive
  -p "p i"         Interpolate over every p-th phase bin, start at i
  -poln 0/1        Delete the specified polarization

Automatic zapping algorithms:
  -r               Zap channels using median smoothed difference
  -R size          Set the size of the median smoothing window
  -l subint        Mow the lawn of the specified subint
  -L               Mow the lawn of all subints
  -d               Zero weight chans using mean offset rejection
  -C cutoff        Zero weight chans based on S/N (std optional)
  -P stdfile       Use this standard profile
  -o cutoff        Zero weight subints with negative dropouts
  -8               Fix ATNF WBCORR 8 bin problem (see also -p)
  -b               Remove variable baseline

The format of the kill file used with the -k option is simply
a list of channel numbers, separated by white space

The cutoff S/N value used with -C is largely arbitrary. You will
need to experiment to find the best value for your archives

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/paz for more details

commands.long: Concatenate multiple archives into one. The default is to append in time; -F appends in frequency.
commands.name: psradd

flags.v: metafile contains list of archive filenames
write files with a new extension
write files to a new directory
Load and install new ephemeris from 'f.eph'
Tscrunch result after each new file added
Tscrunch subints to this length after each new file added
Append data in the frequency direction
Patch missing sub-integrations: domain = time or phase
Append archives to the specified file
Phase align archive with total before adding
Leave amplitudes alone, but update metadata
Omit data if phase offset from total > phath
Test mode: make no changes to file system
Force append against all conventional wisdom
Add archives only with this centre frequency
Only add archives that have integration length > 0
Only add archives that are time (+/- 0.5) seconds long
Do not apply model, ignore phase alignment
... next archive is from different 'sec' division of MJD

commands.help_raw:
psradd - combines archives together

usage: psradd [options] filename[s] 

where options are:

  -h,-help            print this help page
  -i,-version         version information
  -q                  quiet mode
  -v                  verbose mode
  -V                  very verbose mode
  -M metafile         metafile contains list of archive filenames
  -config file        configuration file

Preprocessing options:
  -j commands         execute pulsar shell preprocessing commands
  -J script           execute pulsar shell preprocessing script

Output options:
  -e ext              write files with a new extension
  -O path             write files to a new directory
  -o fname            output result to 'fname'

General options:
  -E f.eph            Load and install new ephemeris from 'f.eph'
  -T                  Tscrunch result after each new file added
  -tsub seconds       Tscrunch subints to this length after each new file added
  -R                  Append data in the frequency direction
  -m domain           Patch missing sub-integrations: domain = time or phase
  -n                  Patch missing frequency channels
  -inplace filename   Append archives to the specified file
  -P                  Phase align archive with total before adding
  -const_phase        Leave amplitudes alone, but update metadata
  -phath turns        Omit data if phase offset from total > phath
  -L                  Log results in <source>.log
  -t                  Test mode: make no changes to file system

Restrictions:
  -F                  Force append against all conventional wisdom
  -r freq             Add archives only with this centre frequency
  -z                  Only add archives that have integration length > 0
  -Z time             Only add archives that are time (+/- 0.5) seconds long
  -ip                 Do not apply model, ignore phase alignment

AUTO ADD: tscrunch and unload when ...
  -C turns            ... CAL phase changes by >= turns
  -D sec              ... next archive is from different 'sec' division of MJD
  -G sec              ... time to next archive > 'sec' seconds
  -g sec              ... time spanned exceeds 'sec' seconds
  -I sec              ... integration length exceeds 'sec' seconds
  -S s/n              ... signal-to-noise ratio exceeds 's/n'
  -exp condition      ... "condition" is true
  -autoT              Disable tscrunch before unloading

Note: AUTO ADD options are incompatible with -o and -T

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/psradd for more details

commands.long: Cross-correlate an observation against a standard profile (template) and emit a TOA in tempo2 or pint format.
commands.name: pat

flags.v: List of archive filenames in metafile
Automatically select standard from specified group
Use up to the specified number of harmonics
Do not fscrunch the standard (frequency-dependent template)
Output only positively delayed arrival times
Estimator configuration options in 'cfg' text file
Zap harmonics due to periodic spikes in profile
Load a component model as output by paas
Print only the phase shift and error in turns
Print as pat-like format smjd + dt

commands.help_raw:
pat - Pulsar::Archive timing 
Usage: pat [options] filenames 
  -q               Quiet mode 
  -v               Verbose mode 
  -V               Very verbose mode 
  -i               Show revision information 
  -M metafile      List of archive filenames in metafile 

Preprocessing options:
  -F               Frequency scrunch before fitting 
  -T               Time scrunch before fitting 
  -j job1[,jobN]   Preprocessing job[s] 
  -J jobs          Multiple preprocessing jobs in 'jobs' file 
  -x               Disable default preprocessing 

Fitting options:
  -a stdfiles      Automatically select standard from specified group
  -c               Choose the maximum harmonic 
  -n harmonics     Use up to the specified number of harmonics 
  -P               Do not fscrunch the standard (frequency-dependent template)
  -I nsubband      Output nsubband TOAs with delta-DM (implies -P)
  -D               Denoise standard 
  -d               Output only positively delayed arrival times 
  -e cfg1[,cfgN]   Estimator configuration option[s] 
  -E cfg           Estimator configuration options in 'cfg' text file 
  -g datafile      Gaussian model fitting 
  -P               Enable frequency-dependent template matching 
  -s stdfile       Location of standard profile 
  -S period        Zap harmonics due to periodic spikes in profile 
                   (use of this option implies SIS) 

Matrix template matching options: 
  -p               Enable matrix template matching 
  -o               Output best-fit matrix model parameters 

Algorithm Selection:
  -A name          Select shift algorithm [default: PGS] 
                   PGS = Fourier phase gradient 
                   GIS = Gaussian interpolation 
                   PIS = Parabolic interpolation 
                   ZPS = Zero pad interpolation 
                   SIS = Sinc interpolation of cross-corration function
                   FDM = Fourier domain with Markov chain Monte Carlo 
                   COF = Centre of Flux 
                   RVM = Rotating Vector Model magnetic meridian 

  -m filename      Load a component model as output by paas 

Output options:
  -f "fmt <flags>" Select output format [default: parkes]
                   Available formats: parkes tempo2, itoa, princeton 
                   For tempo2, <flags> include i = display instrument 
                                               r = display receiver   
                                            IPTA = all IPTA-format flags
  -G char          Format/flag separator char for -f [default: space]
  -C "<options>"   Select vap-like options to be displayed on output 
  -C "<subint> <chan>" Print subint and/or channel                    
  -X "text"        Extra TOA-line text to add (if using tempo2 format)
  -r               Print reference phase and dt 
  -R               Print only the phase shift and error in turns 
  -u               Print as pat-like format smjd + dt 
  -b               Output profile residuals 

Plotting options (if compiled with pgplot):
  -K               Specify plot device
  -t               Plot template, profile and difference 

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/pat for more details

commands.long: Fit a sum of Gaussian components to a high-S/N profile. The output noise-free model becomes the timing template.
commands.name: paas

flags.v: Read model from file        [Default: use empty model]
Report width as standard deviation instead of concentration
log(height) -> force all component heights to be > 0
Set parameters in/out of fit, e.g. -F 011101 fits only
Fit derivative of profile instead (i.e. weight by harm. num.)
Plot results on specified PGPLOT device
Plot with lines instead of steps
Sum groups of "factor" adjacent bins before plotting results
Write analytic standard to given filename [Default: paas.std]
Rotate profile to place peak at bin 0
Fix the relative phase of the components as in pat
Rotate profile by specified number of turns
Zoom in on pulse phase region
After loading a model, rotate it to align to profile
Output details to given filename    [Default: paas.txt]

commands.help_raw:
paas: Pulsar archive analytic standard maker
Usage: paas [options] archive
Input: an archive [and optionally a model]
Output: lekkere chocolade eiren
Options:  
  -r filename   Read model from file        [Default: use empty model]
  -w filename   Write model to file         [Default: paas.m] 
  -S            Report width as standard deviation instead of concentration 
  -c "a b c"    Add component (a=centre, b=concentration, d=height)
  -f            Fit model to pulse profile  
  -L            log(height) -> force all component heights to be > 0 
  -F flags      Set parameters in/out of fit, e.g. -F 011101 fits only
                   for concentration_1, height_1, centre_2 and height_2
                   [Default: fit all parameters]
  -W            Fit derivative of profile instead (i.e. weight by harm. num.)
  -t threshold  Delta_rms/rms fit convergence criterion [Default: 1e-6]
  -d device     Plot results on specified PGPLOT device
  -D            Plot results on PGPLOT device /xs
  -l            Plot with lines instead of steps
  -b factor     Sum groups of "factor" adjacent bins before plotting results
  -s filename   Write analytic standard to given filename [Default: paas.std]
  -i            Interactively select components 
  -C            Centre profile using ephemeris
  -p            Rotate profile to place peak at bin 0
  -P            Fix the relative phase of the components as in pat 
  -R phase      Rotate profile by specified number of turns
  -z ph1,ph2    Zoom in on pulse phase region 
  -a            After loading a model, rotate it to align to profile
  -j filename   Output details to given filename    [Default: paas.txt]

commands.long: Wavelet-based smoothing of a high-S/N profile to produce a low-noise standard. Faster than paas when the profile is well-resolved.
commands.name: psrsmooth

flags.v: metafile contains list of archive filenames
Only print the smoothed number of harmonics

commands.help_raw:
psrsmooth - Denoise profiles using adaptive smoothing

usage: psrsmooth [options] filename[s] 

where options are:

  -h,-help       print this help page
  -i,-version    version information
  -q             quiet mode
  -v             verbose mode
  -V             very verbose mode
  -M metafile    metafile contains list of archive filenames
  -config file   configuration file
  -W             Use Wavelet smoothing (default Sinc)
  -t type      * Use specified wavelet type (default UD8)
  -c factor    * Set wavelet cutoff factor (default 1.3)
  -n             Normalize smoothed profile
  -e ext         Append extention to output (default .sm)
  -H             Only print the smoothed number of harmonics

Options marked with '*' have extra help available via '-help=option'.

commands.long: Print or modify header parameters of an archive: source name, DM, RM, ephemeris, observation type.
commands.name: psredit

flags.v: metafile contains list of archive filenames
write files with a new extension
write files to a new directory
one or more commands, separated by commas
one or more extensions to be added, separated by commas
one or more extensions to be removed, separated by commas
separate elements of a container using delimiter

commands.help_raw:
psredit - query and/or set archive attributes

usage: psredit [options] filename[s] 

where options are:

  -h,-help          print this help page
  -i,-version       version information
  -q                quiet mode
  -v                verbose mode
  -V                very verbose mode
  -M metafile       metafile contains list of archive filenames
  -config file      configuration file

Output options:
  -m                modify (overwrite) the original file
  -e ext            write files with a new extension
  -O path           write files to a new directory

  -c command[s]     one or more commands, separated by commas
  -C filename       read commands from file
  -a extension[s]   one or more extensions to be added, separated by commas
  -r extension[s]   one or more extensions to be removed, separated by commas
  -H                more detailed help
  -Q                do not prefix output with 'keyword='
  -d delim          separate elements of a container using delimiter

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/psredit for more details

commands.long: Compute summary statistics — S/N, weights, on-pulse window — and print them in machine-readable form.
commands.name: psrstat

flags.v: metafile contains list of archive filenames
* expressions to be evaluated and printed
read expressions to be evaluated from file
separate elements of a container using delimiter

commands.help_raw:
psrstat - prints pulsar attributes and statistics

usage: psrstat [options] filename[s] 

where options are:

  -h,-help          print this help page
  -i,-version       version information
  -q                quiet mode
  -v                verbose mode
  -V                very verbose mode
  -M metafile       metafile contains list of archive filenames
  -config file      configuration file

Preprocessing options:
  -j commands       execute pulsar shell preprocessing commands
  -J script         execute pulsar shell preprocessing script

  -c exp[s]       * expressions to be evaluated and printed
  -C filename       read expressions to be evaluated from file
  -l name=<range> * loop over the range of the named parameter
  -Q                do not prefix output with 'keyword='
  -d delim          separate elements of a container using delimiter

Options marked with '*' have extra help available via '-help=option'.

Multiple expressions and/or index ranges may be specified by using 
the -c and/or -l options multiple times. 

For the complete list of all available parameters in an archive, 
run "psrstat <filename>" without any command line options.

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/psrstat for more details

commands.long: Print compact summaries of archive headers — one line per file. The first stop when inspecting a directory of `.ar` files.
commands.name: vap
commands.help_raw:
A program for getting Pulsar::Archive attributes
Usage:
     vap -c PAR [-c PAR ...] [-E] [-p] filenames
Where:

PAR is a string containing one or more parameter names, separated by
    commas.
    If any whitespace is required, then the string containing it must
    be enclosed in quotation marks. Multiple parameters may also be
    specified by using multiple -c options.

    vap -c name,freq

    will print the source name and centre frequency.
    Note that parameter names are case insensitive.
    For a full list of parameter names, type "vap -H"

-n  suppress outputting headers when doing -c

-E  is used to print the most recent ephemeris in an archive

-p  is used to print the set of polynomial coefficients

-P  print the processing history as comma-separated values
-M  extract list of files from metafile

-R  show relative path names

-s  show the extensions present in an archive

-f  format the output into a table with aligned columns and headers
    (overrides -n)

commands.long: Apply calibration solutions (Jones matrices) to convert raw Stokes into the on-sky polarisation state.
commands.name: pac

flags.v: Search for CAL files in the specified path
Add to file extensions recognized in search
Write a new database summary file
Create database from calibrators listed in metafiles
Use the calibrator in filename, as output by pcm/pacv
Use the specified model of the calibrator solution
Use the specified receiver parameters file
Correct ionospheric Faraday rotation using IRI
Derive calibrator Stokes parameters from fluxcal data
Reject outliers when computing CAL levels
Disable calibration of G (applies only when -A is used)
Replace GD with the Jones matrices, J(t,f) in filename
Replace P with the Jones matrices, J(t,f) in filename
Fix the reference degree of polarization
Take closest sky coordinates (no maximum distance)
Do not try to match instruments
Do not try to match frequencies
Do not try to match bandwidths
Fscrunch data to match number of channels of calibrator
Normalize profile weights by absolute gain
File from which input filenames are read
Flip the sign of Stokes Q, U, or V
Path to which output files are written

commands.help_raw:
Calibrates polarization, based on the model described in van Straten (2025). 
Where the instrumental response, M = Phi G D C P F (equation 43), 
pac calibrates Phi G D C and P, where 
 Phi is the phase convention correction 
   G is the complex receiver gain matrix 
   D [optional] describes deviations from an ideal feed (enabled by -S) 
   C is the nominal feed response 
   P is the projection to the celestial sphere 

Usage: pac [options] filenames
  -q             Quiet mode
  -v             Verbose mode
  -V             Very verbose mode

Database options: 
  -d database    Read calibration database summary 
  -p path        Search for CAL files in the specified path 
  -u ext         Add to file extensions recognized in search 
                 (defaults: .cf .pcal .fcal .pfit) 
  -w             Write a new database summary file 
  -W             Create database from calibrators listed in metafiles 
  -k filename    Output database to filename 
  -l             Cache last calibrator

Calibrator options: 
  -A filename    Use the calibrator in filename, as output by pcm/pacv 
  -C model       Use the specified model of the calibrator solution 
  -r filename    Use the specified receiver parameters file 
  -s             Use the Polar Model 
  -I             Correct ionospheric Faraday rotation using IRI 
  -i             Perform the inverse of calibration 
  -x             Derive calibrator Stokes parameters from fluxcal data
  -y             Always trust the Pointing::feed_angle attribute 
  -K sigma       Reject outliers when computing CAL levels 

Calibrator switches: 
  -S             Enable calibration of D 
  -N             Disable calibration of Phi (backend) 
  -P             Disable absolute flux calibration 
  -R             Disable calibration of G (applies only when -A is used) 
  -X             Disable calibration of GD 
  -U             Disable calibration of CP (frontend) 
  -Q filename    Replace GD with the Jones matrices, J(t,f) in filename 
  -Y filename    Replace P with the Jones matrices, J(t,f) in filename 

Rough Alignment options [not recommended]: 
  -B             Fix the off-pulse baseline statistics 
  -D             Fix the reference degree of polarization 

Matching options: 
  -m [b|a]       Use only calibrator before|after observation 
  -T             Take closest time (no maximum interval) 
  -c             Take closest sky coordinates (no maximum distance) 
  -Z             Do not try to match instruments 
  -F             Do not try to match frequencies 
  -b             Do not try to match bandwidths 
  -o             Allow opposite sidebands 
  -a             Per-channel matching 
  -g             Fscrunch data to match number of channels of calibrator 
  -L             Print verbose matching information 

Experimental options: 
  -f             Override flux calibration flag 
  -G             Normalize profile weights by absolute gain 

Input/Output options: 
  -e ext         Extension added to output filenames (default .calib) 
  -j job1[,jobN] Preprocessing job[s] 
  -J jobs        Multiple preprocessing jobs in 'jobs' file 
  -M meta        File from which input filenames are read 
  -n [q|u|v]     Flip the sign of Stokes Q, U, or V 
  -O path        Path to which output files are written 

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/pac for more details

commands.long: Fit a Mueller-matrix calibration model from a set of noise-diode observations.
commands.name: pcm

commands.help_raw:
pcm - polarimetric calibration modelling

usage: pcm [options] filename[s] 

where options are:

  -h,-help           print this help page
  -i,-version        version information
  -M metafile        metafile contains list of archive filenames
  -config file       configuration file

Preprocessing options:
  -j commands        execute pulsar shell preprocessing commands
  -J script          execute pulsar shell preprocessing script
  -V level           set verbosity level [0->4]

Output options:
  -A class           set the output archive class name
  -O path            set directory to which outputs are written
  -out fname         set cal solution output filename (default=pcm.fits)
  -N                 do not unload calibrated data files
  -normalize         normalize output Stokes parameters by total invariant interval
  -noplots           do not plot chosen.ps and onpulse.ps

Input options:
  -C file            filename with list of calibrator files
  -d file            add file to list of calibrator databases
  -W file            filename with list of other data files to be calibrated
  -F days            use flux calibrators within days of pulsar data mid-time
  -L hours           use reference sources within hours of pulsar data mid-time
  -w                 continue if no calibrators are found
  -fa                always trust the pointing:fa (feed angle) attribute
  -va                always trust the pointing:va (vertical/parallactic angle) attribute
  -Z                 ignore the sky coordinates of PolnCal observations
  -solution file     load previous solution from 'file' as first guess
  -fix i,j,k         hold specified parameters fixed

Outlier and step detection options:
  -K sigma           Reject outliers when computing CAL levels
  -calI sigma        Minimum significance of CAL intensity
  -calp frac         Minimum degree of polarization of CAL
  -step sigma        Insert steps where adjacent CAL levels differ

General model configuration options:
  -m model           receiver model name: e.g. bri00e19 or van04e18 [default]
  -e cmd             add measurement equation configuration option
  -E file            load measurement equation configuration options
  -I file            load impurity transformation from file
  -P file            load projection transformations from file
  -projection file   load projection model configuration from YAML file
  -repara            reparallactify the input data
  -iono rm           ionospheric Faraday rotation measure
  -l solver          solver: MEAL [default] or GSL
  -t ncore           solve using ncore threads
  -x                 estimate calibrator Stokes parameters using fluxcal
  -Q                 reference source coupled after frontend
  -R gof             retry solving channels with reduced chisq above gof
  -X gof             flag invalid channels with reduced chisq above gof
  -D name            enable diagnostic: name=report,guess,residual,result,total,invint

MEM: Measurement Equation Modeling 
  -- observations of an unknown source (van Straten 2004)

  -U PAR           * model PAR with a unique value for each CAL
  -u PAR[:W]       * model PAR with a step at each CAL
  -i PAR:MJD       * model PAR with a step at the given epoch (MJD)
  -o PAR:N         * model PAR as polyomial of order N
  -B choose        * set the phase bin selection policy: int, pol, orth, inv
  -c file            choose best input states from observation in file
  -b nbin            add phase bin to constraints
  -n nbin            set the number of phase bins to choose as input states
  -a bins            set the threshold for testing input data phase alignment
  -noalign           disable phase alignment using Archive::centre
  -s                 normalize Stokes parameters by total invariant interval
  -g                 unique absolute gain for each pulsar observation [DEVEL]
  -r                 enforce physically realizable Stokes parameters [DEVEL]

Constraints on degeneracy under commutation 
  -- See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/pcm/degeneracy.shtml 

  -q                 assume that CAL Stokes Q = 0 (linear feeds only)
  -v                 assume that CAL Stokes V = 0 (linear feeds only)
  -k                 assume that the receptors have equal ellipticities
  -z                 assume that the off-pulse baseline has zero Stokes V
  -Y                 model the difference between FluxCal-On and FluxCal-Off
  -f                 model each FluxCal with unique Stokes parameters
  -T code            assume Stokes V [b]oost and/or [r]otation is not degenerate

METM: Measurement Equation Template Matching
  -- observations of a known source as in van Straten (2013) 

  -S file            add filename of calibrated standard
  -G                 fscrunch data to match number of channels of standard
  -H                 automatically choose the number of harmonics
  -n nharm           set the number of harmonics to use as input states
  -1                 solve independently for each observation

Options marked with '*' have extra help available via '-help=option'.

See http://psrchive.sourceforge.net/manuals/pcm for more details

commands.long: Cross-correlate against a template and report flux density per sub-integration / channel.
commands.name: psrflux

flags.v: metafile contains list of archive filenames
Align standard with each profile separately
No fit for profile alignment at all

commands.help_raw:
psrflux - Produce dynamic spectra from Archives

usage: psrflux [options] filename[s] 

where options are:

  -h,-help       print this help page
  -i,-version    version information
  -q             quiet mode
  -v             verbose mode
  -V             very verbose mode
  -M metafile    metafile contains list of archive filenames
  -config file   configuration file

Preprocessing options:
  -j commands    execute pulsar shell preprocessing commands
  -J script      execute pulsar shell preprocessing script

Dynamic spectrum options:
  -e ext         Append extention to output (default .ds)
  -s std         Standard profile file
  -a align       Align standard with each profile separately
  -A noalign     No fit for profile alignment at all
